comment.base: We can condemn violence while still demanding reform. We can support law and order while insisting that power is exercised with restraint, humanity, and respect for life. Empathy should not be selective, and justice should not depend on immigration status, race, or social standing. If we truly want peace, it starts with listening, reforming broken systems, and valuing every life equally. Silence and indifference only guarantee that these cycles continue. My thoughts are with the victim’s family and the community grappling with yet another painful loss. 💔✊🏽
comment.demojified: We can condemn violence while still demanding reform. We can support law and order while insisting that power is exercised with restraint, humanity, and respect for life. Empathy should not be selective, and justice should not depend on immigration status, race, or social standing. If we truly want peace, it starts with listening, reforming broken systems, and valuing every life equally. Silence and indifference only guarantee that these cycles continue. My thoughts are with the victim’s family and the community grappling with yet another painful loss. :broken_heart::raised_fist_medium_skin_tone:

comment.base: Whether on foreign soil or our own streets, this administration's actions have crossed a line. From the unilateral military intervention in Venezuela to the inexcusable killing of an American citizen in Minnesota by federal agents, the pattern is clear: a disregard for sovereignty and human life; plus the continued refusal to release the Epstein files suggests a government protecting its own interests over the truth. The people can no longer remain passive. It is time to stand our ground, demand accountability, and remind this administration that their power is granted by the citizens of the United States of America—not the other way around. We will not be silenced by fear or bureaucracy.

comment.base: Mrs. Good motioned for ice to move past her (she lived there) but instead, they got out of their vehicle, and tried pulling her out of hers, for absolutely no reason. Unmarked vehicles and covered faces IS illegal. Using lethal weapons, as ice agents who ARE NOT law enforcement, IS illegal. Walking to her car, pulling on her door handle, that violates the 4th amendment of unlawful seizure, regardless if successful or not. Especially with this happening before him unaliving her, he lost his qualified immunity. Ice agents cannot force you out of your vehical without a judicial warrant signed by a judge. They cannot force or prevent departure, or do random traffice stops. He positioned himself in front of her vehicle, as she was trying to drive away, when he had multiple chances to de-escalate the situation. it was officer induced jeopardy. His reactions to her actions were not on proportion to the danger of the situation. (Making it illegal, yes.) He had his lethal weapon out before she even started moving her vehicle. He let three rounds go into her, (only 1 bull3t on the far right bottom corner of the windshield, while the rest went into the side of her open driver side window. If she was unalived not by the first bull3t, but by the second, or third...) when she was just trying to leave a dangerous situation that THEY put her in. If the situation was so dangerous, why was he the only one who pulled his weapon? He walked to her car, that had crashed, completely fine. They didn't let a doctor who was at the scene tend to her. When medical care did come? The Ice agents had their cars blocking the road, not moving, and the medics had to WALK to her crashed car. Which again, is illegal. Only to flee the scene afterwards. If you want to say that what she did was illegal, then they could have went to court for it. But she doesn't have that option, thanks to him. She was white. If you think you're safe, no matter what ethnicity you are, you're not. Her unalivement proved that. Don't glorify the situation, when you're being brainwashed to believe Mrs. Good was the bad guy in this situation.
